name: ADMET Batch Prediction
description: Predicts ADMET properties for an entire molecule column using Chemprop deep-learning models and appends results as new columns with color-coded values. Accessible via the main menu or programmatically.

extras.evidence_summary: AdmeticaMenu, getAdmeProperties, and AdmeticaEditor share the same batch prediction workflow; getModels drives model selection for that workflow; Chemprop doc covers the underlying engine.

name: ADMET Info Panel
description: Displays ADMET property predictions for the currently selected molecule in a context-pane panel, with a summary pie chart, color-coded values, and sub-categories including toxicity and drug-likeness.

extras.evidence_summary: Biology | Admetica is a panel widget (role:widgets,panel, domain:chem) for a single molecule; getAdmePropertiesSingle is its compute function; info-panel docs cover the same surface area (drug-likeness, toxicity, structural alerts are ADMET sub-domains).

name: Admetica App
description: A standalone application view (browseable under Chem) for interactively exploring ADMET predictions, including a demo mode that showcases the plugin's main functionality.

extras.evidence_summary: Admetica (role:app, browsePath:Chem) and Admetica Demo (description:Evaluating ADMET properties) share the same name root and represent the app view plus its demo entry point.

name: Hit Triage Integration
description: Integrates ADMET predictions into the Datagrok Hit Triage workflow as a scored triage function, enabling ADMET-based compound prioritization.

extras.evidence_summary: AdmeticaHT has role:hitTriageFunction and its description explicitly states it runs ADMET predictions for Hit Triage.

name: Alation Catalog Browser
description: Embedded Datagrok app that connects to a configured Alation data catalog instance, letting users browse catalogued data sources, tables, and queries and bring them into Datagrok workflows.

extras.evidence_summary: The plugin exposes exactly one user-facing entry point — an app function named 'Alation' — and all changelog entries and docs describe improvements to this single catalog integration.

name: DataFrame Creation and I/O
description: Demonstrates creating DataFrames from CSV, JSON, objects, columns, and raw bytes, as well as saving, loading, comparing, appending, joining, and linking tables.

extras.evidence_summary: All scripts share the theme of constructing or persisting a DataFrame from/to an external format, or combining tables.

name: Column Operations
description: Shows how to add, access, type-convert, sort, and manipulate DataFrame columns, including virtual columns and image columns.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts share column-level CRUD, type conversion, and ordering operations on a DataFrame.

name: Row Filtering and Bitsets
description: Covers row selection, bitset boolean operations (AND/OR/XOR), aggregation, grouping, iterable rows, and collaborative filter-state synchronisation.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts cluster around the BitSet/filter API: row selection, logical set operations, grouping, and multi-user filter state.

name: Metadata and Semantic Types
description: Demonstrates tagging columns and DataFrames with metadata, detecting and assigning semantic types, qualified numbers, value-matchers, and custom category ordering.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts cover the column metadata / semantic-type subsystem: tags, semtypes, category order, date formats, and value pattern matchers.

name: Grid Cell Rendering
description: Shows how to implement custom cell renderers, HTML cells, sparklines, format formulas, tooltips, hit-testing, and paint callbacks for the main data grid.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts all operate inside the grid cell/row rendering pipeline — custom renderers, HTML cells, tooltips, sparklines, and hit-testing.

name: Grid Column Configuration
description: Covers column width, visibility, ordering, color-coding, styles, data formatting, controlled values, and scroll positioning in the data grid.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts configure per-column properties of the grid: size, visibility, order, color, format, and scroll position.

name: Built-in Viewers
description: Samples for every built-in Datagrok viewer: scatter plot, bar/box/line/pie chart, histogram, heat map, trellis, network diagram, globe, markup, form, statistics, and more.

extras.evidence_summary: One-to-one mapping between script names and viewer class names; scripts instantiate, configure, and display each viewer type.

name: Viewer Interaction and Customisation
description: Covers dynamic viewer creation, selection/filter/highlight coordination, custom scatter-plot rendering, filter widgets, and viewer event handling.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts demonstrate programmatic viewer control beyond simple instantiation: events, custom renderers, and filter widget APIs.

name: View Management and Navigation
description: Shows how to open, navigate, and manage application views, including docking, ribbons, toolbox pages, layouts, layout permissions, and virtual views.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts share the application shell / view-lifecycle API: open views, dock panels, configure ribbons and toolbox, save layouts.

name: UI Widgets
description: A catalogue of atomic UI controls: buttons, menus, dropdowns, markdown, icons, images, progress indicators, sliders, tabs, and tooltips.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts all demonstrate a single reusable ui.* widget or styling primitive from the JS API.

name: Layout Containers
description: Shows the block, box, panel, flexbox, splitter, and widget-tree layout primitives used to arrange content inside a Datagrok view.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts demonstrate structural layout primitives (block, box, flex, splitter) rather than interactive controls.

name: Dialogs and Input Forms
description: Covers building modal dialogs, wizards, input forms, combo inputs, custom inputs, parameter validation, and master-detail forms using the JS API.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts all build on the ui.dialog / ui.input family of APIs for collecting user parameters.

name: Event Handling
description: Shows how to subscribe to and prevent platform events: table, layout, function, and UI events.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts explicitly demonstrate the grok.events / onEvent subscription API across different platform subsystems.

name: Function Registry
description: Covers registering, discovering, calling, and caching registered functions; attaching handlers, markup handlers, and auto-renderers; and managing init hooks.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts revolve around the grok.functions registry API: registration, discovery, handlers, auto-renderers, and caching.

name: Package Scripting
description: Shows how to write multi-language scripts (Python, R, JS) within a package, access file storage from scripts, inspect the scripting context, and handle errors.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts share the server-side scripting context (Python/R/JS scripts, file access from scripts, current-object API, error inspection).

name: Server API (dapi)
description: Shows how to use grok.dapi to save/load entities, fetch external URLs, manage user groups, inspect the current user, and persist user settings.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts all call grok.dapi.* endpoints for entity CRUD, user/group management, external fetch, and settings persistence.

name: Projects and Spaces
description: Shows how to list, open, and navigate projects and spaces via the JS API.

extras.evidence_summary: Three scripts covering the complete projects/spaces API surface: list, open, navigate.

name: File Management
description: Demonstrates reading files, checking file existence, and accessing the platform file store from JS scripts.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts cover the file-storage API surface: existence check, read, and directory listing.

name: Database Queries
description: Shows ad-hoc, parameterized, and dynamic SQL queries against Postgres and MSSQL connections, including in-place DB editing and master-detail query patterns.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts and DataQuery nodes all target database connections (Postgres/MSSQL) and cover the full query API: ad-hoc, parameterized, dynamic, and editing.

name: Cheminformatics
description: Samples for the Chem JS API: molecule rendering, substructure search (SMARTS/molfile/ALS), sketcher, R-group decomposition, MCS, diversity search, and scaffold alignment.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts all call the Chem plugin API (substructure search, rendering, sketcher, scaffold tools) using SMILES/SMARTS/molfile inputs.

name: Machine Learning
description: Covers ML API samples: PCA, clustering, missing-value imputation, predictive model training, random data generation, and statistical correlation.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts call grok.functions.call() against ML plugin functions (PCA, KMeans, RF, etc.) and server-side computation.

name: Application Demos
description: Full mini-application examples (stock-broker, bioreactor, general demo) that wire together multiple API surfaces into a cohesive interactive workflow.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts compose multiple API surfaces into domain-specific demo applications rather than showcasing a single API.

name: DataFrame and Column API
description: Core API for creating, accessing, and comparing DataFrames and Columns, including typed accessors, demo-table helpers, and assertion utilities used across the test suite.

extras.evidence_summary: All members deal with DataFrame/Column creation, access, and equality assertion, sharing the Table/Column/Cell/expect naming root.

name: Function Output Annotations
description: Tests for the output-annotation system that controls how function results join or replace existing DataFrame, Column, or ColumnList outputs.

extras.evidence_summary: Seven functions share the testOutputAnnotation/testOutput naming root and systematically cover join-vs-replace semantics across Df/Col/ColList output types.

name: Vector Functions
description: Tests for the JS API's vectorized column-computation mechanism, covering both fully vectorizable and partially non-vectorizable parameter configurations.

extras.evidence_summary: Exact vectorFunc/vectorScript name-root match and the symmetric NonVectorizableParam pair confirm these four members test the same vector-function API surface.

name: Package Function Infrastructure
description: Baseline dummy functions, scripts, and a query that verify fundamental package-level registration, async invocation, and default-value handling in the JS API.

extras.evidence_summary: Shared 'dummy'/'Package' name root across function, script, and query signals deliberate baseline stubs for package-API smoke-testing.

name: Semantic Type Detection
description: Tests the registration and invocation of custom semantic-type detectors that classify column content at runtime.

extras.evidence_summary: The only function with role='semTypeDetector' in meta, confirming it is the semantic type detector registration test fixture.

name: Script Engine and Property Parsing
description: Tests for the script execution engine, covering property annotation parsing, formula evaluation, NaN output handling, and package-level property defaults.

extras.evidence_summary: FormulaScript, propertyParsing, and Test NaN output each probe distinct aspects of script-engine behavior (formula eval, property annotation, edge-case output); the setOptions changelog entry belongs to the same engine-configuration surface.

name: UI Input Forms and Dialogs
description: Tests for the Datagrok dialog and input-form system, including custom input renderers, multi-field forms, and dialog lifecycle validation.

extras.evidence_summary: CustomStringInput, InputFormTest*, and the two dialog doc pages all exercise the same dialog/input-form surface of the JS API.

name: File and Connector API
description: Tests for the dapi.files API and connector framework, covering local file functions, file-connector queries, and web connector integration.

extras.evidence_summary: FileFuncTest, file-connector doc, web-connector doc, and the changelog entry for dapi.files.list tests all belong to the file and connector access surface.

name: Data Query API
description: Tests for building and running database queries via the JS API, including join-based table queries and Elasticsearch integration.

extras.evidence_summary: build-query-test and elastic-search-test docs share the query-execution theme and are the primary API surface covered by this feature.

name: Data Transformation and Table Operations
description: Tests for data normalization, sorting, color coding, and the transformation editor — the core non-scripted table manipulation surface.

extras.evidence_summary: All four test pages exercise table-level data manipulation (transform, normalize, sort, color code) that form a coherent data-wrangling cluster.

name: Data Export
description: Tests for exporting tables and views to various file formats through the JS API.

extras.evidence_summary: Single dedicated export-test doc covers the export surface; isolated but distinct enough from transformation to merit its own feature.

name: Projects and Collaboration
description: Tests for the project browser, sharing, user registration, and inviting collaborators — the multi-user collaboration surface of Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: projects-browser, project-gallery, recently-open, inviting-a-friend, and registration docs collectively cover the collaboration lifecycle (create, share, invite, register).

name: UI Navigation and Metadata
description: Tests for platform-level UI navigation including the help system, menu visibility controls, entity tags, favorites, and entity collections.

extras.evidence_summary: Help, menu visibility, tags/favorites, and collections are all platform navigation and metadata management features grouped under the UI shell surface.

name: Substructure Filter
description: Integration test for the chemical substructure filter, verifying that structure-based column filtering works end-to-end in the platform.

extras.evidence_summary: Single test doc for substructure filter, a chem-domain feature isolated from all other API surface areas in this package.

name: Parquet File Import/Export
description: Lets users open, read, and save Datagrok DataFrames as Apache Parquet files, with WASM-based columnar encoding/decoding. Registered as a platform file handler for .parquet files and as a file exporter menu entry.

extras.evidence_summary: fromParquet/toParquet/parquetFileHandler/saveAsParquet all share the 'parquet' name root, ext='parquet' meta, and changelog history; parquetInit is the autostart WASM loader that backs them.

name: Feather File Import/Export
description: Lets users open, read, and save Datagrok DataFrames as Feather (Arrow IPC stream) files. Registered as a platform file handler for .feather files and as a file exporter menu entry.

extras.evidence_summary: fromFeather/toFeather/featherFileHandler/saveAsFeather share the 'feather' name root and ext='feather' meta, covering the full read-write lifecycle for the Arrow IPC format.

name: Benchling App
description: Top-level Datagrok application and tree-browser panel that surfaces all Benchling LIMS data through a unified UI entry point under the Chem menu.

extras.evidence_summary: Both functions share the 'Benchling' name root and explicit app/appTreeBrowser roles, plus the package-level readme and developer guide describe the same entry point.

name: Sequence Management
description: Read and create amino-acid (AA) and DNA sequences stored in Benchling, returning structured dataframes for downstream analysis in Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: All four functions share the 'AA Sequence' / 'DNA Sequence' name root and form a symmetric Get/Create CRUD pair for biological sequences.

name: DNA Oligo Management
description: Retrieve and register short DNA oligonucleotide entries in Benchling, enabling oligo inventory and design workflows from within Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: Functions share the 'DNA Oligo' name root with a Get/Create CRUD pattern, backed by platform oligo-toolkit and oligonucleotide-design help pages.

name: Assay Data Access
description: Fetch and create assay results and assay runs from Benchling, allowing scientists to pull experimental outcome data into Datagrok for visualization and analysis.

extras.evidence_summary: All four functions share the 'Assay' name root and form symmetric Get/Create CRUD pairs for Benchling assay objects.

name: Molecule Registry
description: Search and register small-molecule entities in the Benchling registry, bridging Benchling compound records with Datagrok chemical analytics.

extras.evidence_summary: Get/Create Molecule pair with shared 'Molecule' name root targeting the Benchling registry, placed under the Chem browse path of the app.

name: Plate Management
description: Query and create lab plates in Benchling, giving Datagrok users visibility into physical sample containers and enabling plate-based workflows.

extras.evidence_summary: Get/Create Plate pair shares the 'Plate' name root; platform Plates package docs are loosely related as they describe analogous plate concepts.

name: Mixture Management
description: Retrieve and create mixture (formulation) records from Benchling, supporting compound formulation and sample preparation tracking inside Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: Get/Create Mixture pair shares the 'Mixture' name root targeting the Benchling Mixtures API.

name: Project Management
description: List Benchling projects accessible to the authenticated user, enabling project-scoped browsing and filtering of LIMS entities within Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: Get Projects function shares the 'Projects' name root with internal test and tutorial docs for project browsing; the deprecated create-project page is weakly related.

name: Macromolecule Detection & Notation Refinement
description: Automatically detects macromolecule semantic types (DNA, RNA, peptides) in columns and refines their notation (HELM, FASTA, BILN, separator) using configurable detectors.

extras.evidence_summary: All share the macromolecule semTypeDetector / notationRefiner role tags and the detect/refine naming pattern; the Bio guide doc covers detection as a foundational capability.

name: Sequence Cell Rendering
description: Renders macromolecule sequence columns in the Datagrok grid with notation-specific cell renderers (FASTA, HELM, BILN, separator, custom) and per-column tooltips and header panels.

extras.evidence_summary: All carry the cellRenderer role tag and are named after the notation they render; longSeqTable* functions are dev test beds for long-sequence rendering in each notation.

name: Sequence Difference Rendering
description: Compares two macromolecule sequence columns to build a per-position difference representation and renders it via a dedicated difference cell renderer.

extras.evidence_summary: MacromoleculeDifferenceCellRenderer and Compare Sequences share explicit naming and description around per-position sequence comparison; sequenceAlignment provides the underlying pairwise alignment.

name: Notation Conversion
description: Converts macromolecule columns between supported sequence notations (FASTA, HELM, BILN, separator, custom) via an interactive conversion dialog.

extras.evidence_summary: Convert Notation and convertDialog share the same name root and dialog entry point; the Polytool doc covers multi-notation polymer workflows that this feature enables.

name: Molecule to HELM Conversion
description: Converts small-molecule structures (molfiles / SDF) to HELM notation by matching atoms against a monomer library, enabling peptide enumeration workflows.

extras.evidence_summary: Molecules to HELM, Molecule to HELM Single, getMolFromHelm, and molToHelmConverterPy all implement the molecule-to-HELM pipeline; SDF to JSON Library converts SDF monomer inputs for this workflow.

name: Atomic Level Structure
description: Expands macromolecule sequences (HELM / FASTA / BILN) into full atomic-level molblocks and displays 2D / 3D molecular structures in the property panel.

extras.evidence_summary: All share the 'To Atomic Level' / seq2atomic name root and the same pipeline of converting macromolecule sequences to molblocks for 2D / 3D visualization; demoBioAtomicLevel is the demo entry point.

name: File Import / Export (FASTA, BAM)
description: Imports FASTA / nucleotide files and BAM alignment files directly into Datagrok tables and exports sequence columns back to FASTA format.

extras.evidence_summary: importFasta, importBam, and saveAsFasta carry fileHandler / fileExporter roles; oligo-toolkit and oligonucleotide-design docs cover the nucleotide-file import use case.

name: Monomer Library Management
description: Provides a full CRUD UI to manage HELM monomer libraries and collections, including browsing, editing monomers, matching molecules to monomers, and standardising library JSON.

extras.evidence_summary: All share 'Monomer' / 'Library' name roots or the Manage Monomer Libraries menu path, covering the full lifecycle of HELM monomer library management from browse to edit to standardise.

name: Split to Monomers
description: Splits a macromolecule sequence column into individual per-position monomer columns, enabling position-level analysis of peptide libraries.

extras.evidence_summary: Split to Monomers and SplitToMonomersEditor share the same name and editor role; getHelmMonomers provides per-column monomer extraction used by the split operation.

name: Get Region
description: Extracts a named subsequence region (e.g. CDR loop, variable domain) from a macromolecule column into a new column, via an interactive panel, top-menu action, or region input widget.

extras.evidence_summary: All share the 'Get Region' name root or describe sub-region extraction; Sequence Column Input exposes region selection as a reusable input widget; *App functions are dev test entry points.

name: WebLogo & Composition Analysis
description: Visualizes the positional monomer composition of a sequence set as a WebLogo plot, with aggregate and per-row composition panels.

extras.evidence_summary: WebLogo, both Composition Analysis variants, and the two demo apps all share the WebLogo / composition naming; the help doc directly documents this viewer.

name: Multiple Sequence Alignment
description: Aligns a set of macromolecule sequences using built-in engine or PepSeA (MAFFT-based) alignment algorithms, including support for non-canonical peptides and HELM.

extras.evidence_summary: MSA, Multiple Sequence Alignment, and PepSeA all carry sequenceMSA roles; read-tree-pkl reads phylogenetic clustering trees produced alongside MSA; Sequence generator creates synthetic test data.

name: Sequence Space
description: Projects macromolecule sequences into a 2D scatter plot via dimensionality reduction of pairwise distances, enabling visual exploration of chemical diversity.

extras.evidence_summary: Sequence Space, sequenceSpaceTransform, and SequenceSpaceEditor implement the dim-reduction pipeline; Encode Sequences carries the 'dim-red-preprocessing-function' role; Embed script does the numeric embedding; demoBioSequenceSpace is the demo.

name: Sequence Activity Cliffs
description: Detects pairs of macromolecule sequences that are structurally similar but have a large difference in a measured biological activity, highlighting SAR discontinuities.

extras.evidence_summary: All five function IDs share the 'seqActivityCliffs' / 'Sequence Activity Cliffs' name; Peptides SAR doc covers the SAR / activity cliff analysis workflow for macromolecules.

name: Sequence Similarity & Diversity Search
description: Interactive viewers to find the most similar sequences to a query and to pick the most diverse subset from a macromolecule dataset.

extras.evidence_summary: The four viewer functions pair by name (Similarity Search / similaritySearch, Diversity Search / diversitySearch); the demo unifies both into one entry point.

name: Subsequence Search & Substructure Filter
description: Searches for a motif or subsequence within macromolecule columns and provides a grid filter widget to interactively narrow rows by substructure match.

extras.evidence_summary: Subsequence Search / SearchSubsequenceEditor implement motif search; Bio Substructure Filter provides the interactive filter widget using the same underlying matcher.

name: Sequence Identity & Similarity Scoring
description: Computes per-row identity or monomer-fingerprint similarity scores relative to a reference sequence, adding scored result columns to the table.

extras.evidence_summary: Identity, Similarity, and seqIdentity all compute pairwise sequence scores against a reference; Helm Fingerprints generates the monomer-fingerprint vectors consumed by the Similarity scorer.

name: Antibody Numbering & Annotations
description: Assigns standard antibody numbering schemes (IMGT, Kabat, Chothia, AHo) to sequence columns and lets users view and manage CDR / framework region annotations.

extras.evidence_summary: Apply Numbering Scheme and Immunum both assign antibody numbering (IMGT/Kabat/Chothia/AHo) and share the antibodyNumbering role; Manage Annotations exposes the resulting region annotations for review.

name: Liability Scanning
description: Scans macromolecule sequences for chemical liabilities such as deamidation and oxidation sites that affect biologic drug stability.

extras.evidence_summary: Scan Liabilities is the sole explicit liability-scanning entry point, matching changelog entries about deamidation / oxidation detection and separator support.

name: VD Regions Viewer
description: Visualizes V-Domain antibody regions and CDR loops in a dedicated grid viewer, showing aligned region positions across a sequence library.

extras.evidence_summary: VdRegions is the sole V-Domain regions viewer with role viewer,panel, described as 'V-Domain regions viewer' and referenced in the antibody support changelog entry.

name: Sequence Encoding for ML
description: Encodes macromolecule sequences as numeric feature vectors to feed into dimensionality reduction and other machine-learning pipelines.

extras.evidence_summary: Encode Sequences carries the 'dim-red-preprocessing-function' role tag, explicitly marking it as the ML preprocessing step for sequence space and similar dim-red analyses.

name: Demo Biologics Database Setup
description: Initializes and populates the demo Biologics (ADC) database on package start, providing sample data for exploration and demonstration.

extras.evidence_summary: createDemoBiologicsData and autostartbiologics share the same initialization theme; the autostart role ensures demo data is ready when the package loads.

name: ADC Glyph Rendering
description: Generates and populates visual glyph icons (PNG/base64) for ADC molecules, enabling rich visual representation of ADC compounds in grid and info-pane views.

extras.evidence_summary: populateAdcGlyphs is the sole function for ADC visual icon generation; info-pane docs are linked because ADC glyphs surface inside Datagrok info panes.

name: Antibody Profile & Assay Query
description: Queries the biologics database for comprehensive antibody profiles and assay results filtered by organism and target, including HC/LC chain data.

extras.evidence_summary: Both queries retrieve organism/target-scoped antibody and assay data from the same biologics connection; the changelog records a direct update to assaysByOrganism.

name: ADC Activity & IC50 Filtering
description: Finds ADCs that meet specified caspase-activity or IC50 thresholds, supporting compound triage by potency.

extras.evidence_summary: Both queries share the pattern of filtering ADCs by a numeric activity threshold (caspase activity and IC50), forming a cohesive compound-triage feature.

name: ADC-Drug Linkage Query
description: Retrieves ADCs linked to a specified drug identifier, enabling drug-centric ADC exploration across the biologics database.

extras.evidence_summary: adcsLinkedToDrug has a distinct drug-linkage lookup pattern that does not fit the activity-threshold or antibody-profile features.

name: Dose-Response Curve Retrieval
description: Retrieves the dose-response curve JSON for a biologics assay result by its GROKCRV-###### identifier, enabling programmatic access to curve-fitting data.

extras.evidence_summary: getBiologicsCurveByAssayResultIdentifier provides structured-ID point-lookup of dose-response curve JSON, distinct from the organism/target-scoped assay queries.

name: Peptide HELM Sequence Retrieval
description: Retrieves the HELM sequence notation for a biologics peptide by its GROKPEP-###### identifier, supporting sequence-level inspection of peptide therapeutics.

extras.evidence_summary: getBiologicsPeptideHelmByIdentifier is the sole query for peptide HELM notation lookup, a distinct sequence-retrieval concern separate from assay or ADC queries.

name: ESMFold Protein Structure Prediction
description: Predicts the 3D structure of a protein from its amino acid sequence using the ESMFold model via NVIDIA BioNeMo. Results are surfaced as a dataframe and an interactive panel in the Bio context.

extras.evidence_summary: EsmFold function, esmfoldPython script, and Bio|EsmFold widget share the ESMFold name root and collectively implement protein structure prediction from sequence.

name: DiffDock Molecular Docking
description: Predicts the 3D binding pose of a small molecule against a protein target using the DiffDock diffusion model, with a supporting context panel and target-file helper for batch workflows.

extras.evidence_summary: DiffDock, diffDockModelScript, getTargetFiles, diffdockPython, and Biology|DiffDock panel share the DiffDock name root; changelog confirms docking menu naming and info-panel bug fixes.

name: MolMIM Controlled Molecule Generation
description: Generates novel molecules with desired properties using the MolMIM model, supporting controlled latent-space exploration with configurable generation parameters.

extras.evidence_summary: MolMIMModel function and MolMIMGenerate script share the MolMIM name root and together implement the controlled molecule generation workflow.

name: Mol* (Molstar) 3D Structure Viewer
description: Standalone 3D viewer backed by the Mol*/RCSB Molstar engine for large biological molecules with support for cartoon, ball-and-stick, and other molecular representations.

extras.evidence_summary: Viewer registration (role: viewer,panel), programmatic open functions (viewBiostructure/viewPdbById/viewPdbByData), context-menu item, and the dedicated help page all share the Biostructure/Molstar name root.

name: NGL 3D Structure Viewer
description: Standalone 3D viewer backed by the NGL library for large biological molecules, supporting formats such as mmtf, cns, gro, prmtop, and others.

extras.evidence_summary: Viewer function (role: viewer,panel), NGL GL service accessor, context-menu item, and dedicated help page all share the NGL name root.

name: Biotrack Sequence Track Viewer
description: Viewer displaying polymer annotation tracks (secondary structure, sequence features, etc.) for biological sequences using the Saguaro engine.

extras.evidence_summary: Viewer registration (role: viewer,panel) and test apps share the Biotrack/saguaro name root; the combined app shows Molstar + Biotrack used together.

name: In-Grid Structure Cell Renderer
description: Renders 3D protein and small-molecule structure thumbnails directly inside Datagrok grid cells for PDB-ID and chemical structure columns, using both Molstar and NGL backends.

extras.evidence_summary: Both cell renderers (role: cellRenderer) and all in-grid demo/test apps target the same pattern: 3D structure thumbnails rendered inside grid cells.

name: Structure File Import
description: File-handler registrations that allow Datagrok to open a wide variety of 3D structure formats (PDB, mmCIF, GRO, XYZ, mmtf, ply, obj, ccp4, and more) directly from the file browser.

extras.evidence_summary: Import functions all carry role: fileHandler with ext metadata covering the same domain of structure file formats; readAsText helpers support file-content loading.

name: Structure File Preview
description: File-browser preview panels that render structure, surface, density, and topology biostructure files inline when a user inspects a file in Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: All six functions carry role: fileViewer and share the preview+Ngl/Biostructure naming pattern, covering different sub-formats of the same file-preview use case.

name: RCSB PDB Structure Fetch
description: Fetches protein structures from the RCSB Protein Data Bank by PDB ID in PDB, mmCIF, or BinaryCIF format, and can bulk-retrieve amino-acid sequences for a whole table column.

extras.evidence_summary: Three RCSB-prefixed data-provider functions share the same retrieval pattern (PDB ID → structure data); Fetch PDB Sequences extends this to table columns; getPdbHelper supports the same PDB-fetch workflow.

name: PDB Context Panel & 3D Structure Widget
description: Context-panel widgets that automatically display a 3D structure preview and PDB metadata when the user selects a row with a PDB-ID or biostructure semantic type, and a panel for opening the residues table.

extras.evidence_summary: Panel/widget functions (role: panel or widgets,panel) surface 3D structure information in the context panel; residue-table functions expose the same structural data in tabular form.

name: Docking Conformations Viewer

extras.evidence_summary: importPdbqt is the file entry point for docking data (description: 'docking result ligand poses'); demoBioDockingConformations and dockingDemo both explicitly describe displaying ligand poses along the structure.

name: BiostructureData Serialization API

extras.evidence_summary: biostructureDataToJson packs BiostructureData to JSON; Copy/Download raw value actions expose the same underlying data for external use; all share the BiostructureData-value concept.

name: Protein Structure Prediction

name: Northwind CRUD Demo
description: An interactive app demonstrating the Cruddy CRUD framework with the classic Northwind relational dataset (customers, orders, products). Registered in the Dev browse path as the primary entry-point demo.

extras.evidence_summary: northwindDemo is the only function with a browsePath ('Dev') and the readme describes the Cruddy framework through this dataset, making them the natural primary demo cluster.

name: ChEMBL CRUD Demo
description: An app demonstrating the Cruddy CRUD framework applied to ChEMBL chemical/bioactivity data, showing entity browsing and editing for chemistry-domain datasets.

extras.evidence_summary: chemblDemo is a self-contained CRUD demo targeting ChEMBL data, distinguished from the Northwind demo by dataset domain and the absence of a registered browsePath.

name: In-Cell Curve Fitting
description: Automatically fits mathematical models (e.g. 4PL dose-response) to series of data points and renders interactive fitted curves directly inside grid cells.

extras.evidence_summary: The Fit cellRenderer, Curve fitting descriptor, and dose-response workflow doc all directly implement and describe in-grid curve fitting; the init and semTypeDetector are infra that exists solely to enable it.

name: Data-to-Curves Transformation
description: Converts raw tabular data (concentration, readout, and metadata columns) into a curve-encoded dataframe with per-compound fitted curves, accessible from the Data top menu.

extras.evidence_summary: Both functions share the 'dataToCurves' name root and represent the same pipeline (bulk transform + its menu entry), tracked consistently as 'Data-to-Curves' across multiple changelog versions.

name: Multi-Curve Viewer
description: A standalone Datagrok viewer that superimposes multiple in-cell curves on one chart, with configurable axes, legend, and outlier display.

extras.evidence_summary: The viewer function and its dedicated help page share the exact name 'Multi curve viewer'; changelog entries track its parameter evolution (showOutliers, legendColumnName, min/max axes) as a distinct viewer feature.

name: Curve Format Import
description: Imports fitted curves from external file formats — GraphPad Prism PZFX, XML 3DX, and compact dose-response JSON — via a pluggable converter registry, with full file preview and handler support for PZFX.

extras.evidence_summary: All three converter functions share the 'curveConverter' role and identical output type; the PZFX viewer and handler extend this into full file-handling for GraphPad Prism, with changelog noting 'Added PZFX file viewer and handler' and 'Added dynamically extendable format support'.

name: Curve Statistics Columns
description: Extracts statistical parameters (e.g. IC50, Hill slope) from fitted curves and appends them as new dataframe columns, with both per-row and aggregated variants.

extras.evidence_summary: Both transform-role functions append curve-derived statistics to a dataframe and differ only in aggregation level, making them two facets of the same statistics-extraction capability.

name: Minimum Significant Ratio (MSR)
description: Server-side Python analysis that computes the Minimum Significant Ratio for compounds from IC50 values, run dates, and assay metadata to assess inter-run assay reproducibility.

extras.evidence_summary: The Calculate MSR script and its changelog entries ('Added MSR script', 'Updated MSR env') consistently describe this as a self-contained reproducibility-analysis capability distinct from curve fitting itself.

name: Assay Plates
description: Visualizes and manages assay plate data including well-level concentration, layout, and readout information for drug-screening workflows.

extras.evidence_summary: The 'Assay Plates' function (concentration/layout/readout data) and the Plates package docs share a well/plate theme corroborated by changelog entries on plate rendering and well generation.

name: Python Scripting Tests
description: Tests cross-VM execution of Python scripts, covering the full I/O type matrix: primitives, dataframes (including empty and cached), columns, files, blobs, maps, graphics, strings, choices, datetime, and exception handling.

extras.evidence_summary: All scripts share the 'Python' name prefix and the 'python' language tag, covering the standard I/O type matrix repeated for each CVM language.

name: Python Environment Management Tests
description: Tests that the CVM correctly creates, isolates, and reuses Python environments, covering conda-only extras, pip-only deps, mixed conda+pip, numpy ABI compatibility, Python 3.12 pinning, and env reuse detection.

extras.evidence_summary: All six scripts share the 'PythonEnv' name prefix and test distinct Python environment isolation scenarios distinct from basic I/O type coverage.

name: R Scripting Tests
description: Tests cross-VM execution of R scripts covering the full I/O type matrix — primitives, dataframes (including empty), columns, files, blobs, maps, graphics, strings, datetime — plus custom-package environment tests using stringi.

extras.evidence_summary: All scripts share the 'R ' name prefix and are tagged with the 'r' language; two env scripts (Env String Reverse, Env File String Reverse) add R package environment coverage.

name: Octave Scripting Tests
description: Tests cross-VM execution of Octave scripts covering the standard I/O type matrix, with two Octave-specific additions: exponential operator syntax edge case and stdout capture.

extras.evidence_summary: All scripts share the 'Octave' name prefix and 'octave' language tag; Exponential Operator and Stdout scripts add Octave-specific coverage beyond the shared matrix.

name: Julia Scripting Tests
description: Tests cross-VM execution of Julia scripts covering the full I/O type matrix — primitives, dataframes (including empty), integer columns, files, blobs, maps, graphics, strings, and datetime.

extras.evidence_summary: All scripts share the 'Julia' name prefix and 'julia' language tag, covering the standard I/O type matrix for the Julia CVM runtime.

name: JavaScript (Browser) Scripting Tests
description: Tests cross-VM execution of client-side JavaScript scripts covering the standard I/O type matrix — primitives, dataframes, columns, files, maps, graphics, dates, and strings.

extras.evidence_summary: All scripts share the 'JavaScript' name prefix and 'javascript' language tag (client-side, distinct from NodeJS), covering the shared CVM I/O type matrix.

name: NodeJS Scripting Tests
description: Tests cross-VM execution of server-side Node.js scripts covering the full I/O type matrix — primitives, dataframes, columns, files, blobs, maps, graphics, dates, and strings.

extras.evidence_summary: All scripts share the 'NodeJS' name prefix and 'nodejs' language tag (server-side, distinct from browser JavaScript), covering the shared CVM I/O type matrix plus file/blob I/O.

name: ClickHouse Connector Tests
description: Validates ClickHouse JDBC type mapping (UUID, signed/unsigned integers, floats, arrays, tuples, maps, dates, nested, geo) and the full parameterized-pattern operator suite against a live ClickHouse connection.

extras.evidence_summary: All 31 queries share the 'ClickHouseDBTests' connection and the 'ClickHouse' name prefix, covering both type-mapping and pattern-operator verification for this provider.

name: MariaDB Connector Tests
description: Validates MariaDB JDBC type mapping (dates, binary, bit, character, float, integer, spatial) and the full parameterized-pattern operator suite against a live MariaDB connection.

extras.evidence_summary: All 28 queries share the 'MariaDbDBTests' connection and the 'MariaDb' name prefix, covering type mapping and pattern operators for this provider.

name: SQL Server (MSSQL) Connector Tests
description: Validates MSSQL JDBC type mapping (dates, XML, binary, numeric, float, money, geography, geometry) and the full parameterized-pattern operator suite against a live SQL Server connection.

extras.evidence_summary: All 28 queries share the 'MSSQLDBTests' connection and the 'MSSQL' name prefix, covering type mapping (including SQL Server-specific money and spatial types) and pattern operators.

name: MySQL Connector Tests
description: Validates MySQL JDBC type mapping (dates, binary, bit, character, float, JSON, integer, spatial) and the full parameterized-pattern operator suite against a live MySQL connection.

extras.evidence_summary: All 29 queries share the 'MySqlDBTests' connection and the 'MySql' name prefix, covering type mapping (including JSON native type) and pattern operators.

name: Oracle Connector Tests
description: Validates Oracle JDBC type mapping (XML, character, dates, JSON, URI, Varray, numeric, LOB) and the full parameterized-pattern operator suite against a live Oracle connection.

extras.evidence_summary: All 29 queries share the 'OracleDBTests' connection and the 'Oracle' name prefix, covering Oracle-specific types (URI, Varray, LOB) and pattern operators.

name: PostgreSQL Connector Tests — Type Mapping & Pattern Operators
description: Validates PostgreSQL JDBC type mapping for advanced types (array, bit strings, composite, JSONB, UUID, XML, numeric precision, serial sequences) and the full parameterized-pattern operator suite.

extras.evidence_summary: All 38 queries use the 'PostgreSQLDBTests' connection and cover PostgreSQL-specific advanced column types plus the standard pattern-operator matrix.

name: Snowflake Connector Tests
description: Validates Snowflake JDBC type mapping (binary, dates, geo, numeric, semi-structured JSON/VARIANT) and the full parameterized-pattern operator suite against a live Snowflake connection.

extras.evidence_summary: All 26 queries share the 'SnowflakeDBTests' connection and the 'Snowflake' prefix, covering Snowflake-specific types (semi-structured) and pattern operators.

name: Parameterized Query Input Types
description: Tests end-to-end query execution with typed input parameters (int, double, bool, datetime, string choices, string patterns) and realistic relational fixtures (Orders, Products) across MSSQL and PostgreSQL.

extras.evidence_summary: Queries from MSSQLTest, PostgresTest, and PostgreSQLTest connections all follow the ByInt/ByDouble/ByDatetime/Orders/Products naming pattern, testing typed query parameters in realistic scenarios shared across multiple providers.

name: Query Result Caching
description: Tests the platform's table-level and scalar caching layer, connection-scoped caching, cache invalidation, and wide-table cache behavior — all backed by PostgreSQL fixtures.

extras.evidence_summary: All 11 queries contain 'Cache' or 'Cached' in their name, use PostgreSQLDBTests/PostgreSQLDBTestsCached connections, and explicitly test caching semantics (invalidation, scalar vs. table, client vs. server).

name: Result-Set Shape Validation
description: Tests that the connector correctly returns result sets of varying shapes — normal, wide-column, long-row, empty, and simple — ensuring column schemas are preserved even on empty results.

extras.evidence_summary: These five PostgreSQLDBTests queries are named explicitly for table shapes (Normal/Wide/Long) or edge-case result conditions (EmptyResult, SimpleSelect), grouping them as result-set dimensionality tests.

name: Query Performance Benchmarks
description: Measures raw throughput of the PostgreSQL JDBC connector under load, providing a performance baseline for regression detection.

extras.evidence_summary: PostgresPerf uses the 'PostgresTest' connection and the 'Perf' suffix signals it is a performance/load benchmark rather than a functional type or pattern test.

name: Geo-Spatial Type Support (Cross-DB)
description: Cross-provider view of geo and spatial type handling: ClickHouse Geo, MariaDB/MySQL Spatial, MSSQL Geography+Geometry, and Snowflake Geo — verifying that each connector correctly surfaces these types.

extras.evidence_summary: These six queries, each in a different provider feature, all test geospatial column types (Geo/Spatial/Geography/Geometry keywords) and together represent the platform's cross-DB spatial coverage.

name: Package Smoke Test
description: A single query against the System:Datagrok connection that verifies the DBTests package itself loads and can execute a query, serving as a pre-flight health check.

extras.evidence_summary: PackageTest uses the 'System:Datagrok' connection (not a vendor-specific DB), marking it as a package-level bootstrap/smoke test distinct from any provider suite.

name: Test Infrastructure Utilities
description: Core TypeScript helpers used by all DB test suites: expectTable for tabular result assertion, info for package metadata, and changelog entries tracking test-suite maintenance.

extras.evidence_summary: expectTable and info are the only registered TS functions; changelog entries all describe infra-level maintenance (API bumps, test fixes, visibility) rather than new DB provider coverage.

name: Package Documentation
description: The DBTests package-level README (describing purpose and usage) and the third-party credits file.

extras.evidence_summary: Both docs have URLs under /packages/DBTests/ and kinds 'readme'/'credits', making them the authoritative package-level documentation.

name: Internal Platform Test Guides
description: A collection of internal QA guide pages covering platform-wide test scenarios (dialogs, export, sorting, UI, connectors, etc.) that are bundled with DBTests as a reference for internal testers.

extras.evidence_summary: All 23 docs have audience 'internal' and kind 'guide' under /help/_internal/tests/, covering platform UI and connector scenarios that are not DB-provider-specific but are shipped with DBTests for QA reference.

name: Dendrogram Viewer
description: An interactive tree viewer for hierarchical data, supporting zoom, pan, node selection, and row-highlight synced with the grid.

extras.evidence_summary: The viewer function, its backing service, and tree-helper share the 'Dendrogram'/'dendrogramService'/'treeHelper' naming root; the demo apps exercise the viewer directly and the help guide documents it.

name: Hierarchical Clustering
description: Computes hierarchical clustering on tabular data (numeric features, sequences, or molecules) via Python scripts and injects the resulting Newick tree into the grid.

extras.evidence_summary: All four user-callable functions share the 'Hierarchical Clustering' name and description 'injects tree to grid', backed by three Python scripts for distance/tree computation, with two demo apps and a tree-generation dialog.

name: Tree-in-Grid Rendering
description: Renders a dendrogram as a resizable neighbor panel alongside the data grid, with an interactive cut-off slider that assigns rows to clusters.

extras.evidence_summary: Three demo apps named 'treeForGrid*' exercise the custom grid renderer and cut-off slider; changelog entries confirm a resizable neighbor panel, cut-slider indicator, and cluster assignment by cut-off.

name: Newick File Import and Preview
description: Registers file handlers for .nwk/.newick files, enabling import into Datagrok and inline preview of Newick-format phylogenetic trees.

extras.evidence_summary: importNwk (role: fileHandler, ext: nwk/newick) and previewNewick (role: fileViewer, fileViewer: nwk/newick) are both explicitly bound to Newick file extensions with no ambiguity.

name: Test Manager
description: An admin application for running, filtering, and tracking Datagrok package tests across all installed packages, with per-test, per-category, and per-package history queries.

extras.evidence_summary: Named 'Test Manager' app with dedicated tree browser, test-runner functions, three history queries, a matching guide doc, and a changelog dominated by 'TM:' prefix entries covering routing, Dart test integration, state saving, and hierarchy improvements.

name: Developer Panel
description: A sidebar widget that auto-starts when DevTools is loaded, giving plugin developers quick access to developer tooling such as the script runner and object inspection within Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: renderDevPanel widget and autostartTools autostart function both explicitly implement the Dev Panel; changelog entry GROK-13970 fixes the Dev Panel run option by name.

name: Inspector Panel
description: Registers a custom DevTools panel inside the Datagrok object Inspector, exposing developer-relevant properties and actions for the currently selected object.

extras.evidence_summary: Function is a widget with friendly_name 'DevTools' registered as an inspector panel; changelog GROK-13265 specifically introduced the ability to add custom inspector panels.

name: ODE Solver IDE
description: Interactive in-browser IDE for defining, solving, and visualizing systems of ordinary differential equations using a domain-specific language with multiple numerical methods.

extras.evidence_summary: The app entry point, all solve*/solveODE computational functions, the primary help doc, and seven 1.5.0 changelog entries on new numerical methods all converge on the ODE-solver IDE theme.

name: IVP File Format
description: Platform integration for .ivp (initial value problem) files: file-type handler that opens them in Diff Studio and a preview viewer for quick inspection without leaving the file browser.

extras.evidence_summary: ivpFileHandler (role: fileHandler, ext: ipv) and previewIvp (role: fileViewer, fileViewer: ivp) share the ivp file-format name root and complementary file-handling roles.

name: ODE Serialization API
description: Developer-facing API for converting textual ODE definitions into structured objects or executable JavaScript code, enabling programmatic embedding of ODE models.

extras.evidence_summary: serializeEquations ('Return serialized initial value problem') and odesToCode ('Perform ODEs serialization to JS-code') both convert ODE text to alternate representations, forming a paired serialization API.

name: Built-in Domain Models
description: Curated library of ready-to-run ODE models covering pharmacokinetics (PK-PD), bioprocess engineering (Bioreactor, Acid Production), environmental chemistry (Pollution), and ballistics (Ball flight).

extras.evidence_summary: Five functions share the 'model' tag with scientific descriptions; runModel drives any of them via the Diff Studio UI; Demo variants provide interactive samples.

name: Model Hub Browser
description: Tree-based browser for discovering and launching ODE models registered in the Diff Studio model hub, with an improved open-model menu.

extras.evidence_summary: runDiffStudioTreeBrowser is the sole tree-browser entry point; changelog entries for 'Fixed Model Hub issue' (1.5.1) and 'Improved the open model menu' (1.4.7) confirm the feature.

name: Parameter Fitting
description: Optimization of ODE model parameters to fit experimental data, with target column selection, progress tracking, and configurable color scheme — surfaced in the Diff Studio UI, not as a separate registered function.

extras.evidence_summary: Changelog entries across 1.3.3–1.3.5 explicitly name 'the Fitting feature' and 'parameters optimization'; no registered function exists, indicating a UI-only capability.

name: Sensitivity Analysis
description: Analysis of how ODE model outputs change with parameter variation, executed in web workers for non-blocking performance.

extras.evidence_summary: Single changelog entry ('Sensitivity analysis in workers') confirms the feature exists; no registered function exposes it directly, suggesting it is a UI-only sub-tool.

name: AutoDock Vina Batch Docking
description: Docks a column of small-molecule ligands against a protein target using a Docker-backed AutoDock Vina service, returning scored poses for each compound. Exposed as a Hit Triage function so it integrates into Datagrok screening workflows.

extras.evidence_summary: All share the 'AutoDock'/'autodock' name root, the hitTriage role, and the get→dock→results call chain; changelog entries confirm iterative GPU, error-handling, and caching fixes to this same compute path.

name: Docking Application
description: A full-screen interactive application (accessible via the Bio browse path) that lets users configure targets and ligand sets, launch AutoDock runs, and explore docked poses in an integrated 3-D view.

extras.evidence_summary: The 'Docking' app function (role=app, browsePath=Bio) and autoDockApp build the same interactive UI; Demo Docking (description: 'pose visualization') and changelog 'Add demo / Demo improvements' entries confirm this as the primary entry-point experience.

name: Molecule Docking Info Panel
description: Context-sensitive panel widget that runs AutoDock on the currently selected molecule and renders the docked pose inline, giving users per-compound docking feedback while browsing a table.

extras.evidence_summary: getAutodockSingle (role=widget, inputs: semantic_value) and 'Biology | AutoDock' (role=widgets,panel) share the same panel-registration pattern and together deliver single-molecule docking in the info-pane.

name: Substructure Search
description: Panel-based substructure search against the DrugBank Open Structures dataset, returning matching drug compounds for a drawn or pasted molecule.

extras.evidence_summary: Function name explicitly encodes 'Substructure Search' under the Databases > DrugBank menu path, and the readme describes it as a primary capability of the plugin.

name: Similarity Search
description: Panel-based molecular similarity search against the DrugBank Open Structures dataset, displaying similarity scores alongside each matching compound.

extras.evidence_summary: Function name encodes 'Similarity Search' under Databases > DrugBank; the v1.0.16 changelog entry 'Added similarity score for compounds in the similarity panel' directly tracks this feature's evolution.

name: Drug Name to Molecule Lookup
description: Converter that resolves a drug name string to its corresponding molecular structure using the DrugBank dataset.

extras.evidence_summary: The 'Drug Name Molecule' converter function is a standalone name-to-structure lookup, distinct from the search panels and backed by the DrugBank compound registry.

name: Package Initialization
description: Internal bootstrapping routine that initializes the DrugBank plugin on load.

extras.evidence_summary: The init-role function plus developer-facing docs (claude.md, credits.md) describe plugin scaffolding rather than any user-facing capability.

name: Dimensionality Reduction
description: Reduces high-dimensional tabular data to a small number of components via PCA and multi-column projection, with preprocessing adapters for numeric and string columns and an optional UMAP/t-SNE pipeline.

extras.evidence_summary: func:EDA:PCA links directly to /help/explore/dim-reduction#pca; the preprocessing functions carry the dim-red-preprocessing-function role tag, and Multi Column Dimensionality Reduction is the generic wrapper for the whole pipeline.

name: DBSCAN Clustering
description: Density-based spatial clustering of applications with noise (DBSCAN), available as a standalone function on a dataframe and as a post-processing step wired into the dimensionality-reduction pipeline.

extras.evidence_summary: Both functions share the DBSCAN name; one is a standalone clustering function and the other is tagged dim-red-postprocessing-function, forming two entry points for the same algorithm.

name: MCL Clustering
description: Markov Clustering (MCL) groups dataset rows by simulating stochastic flow on a graph, exposed as a compute function, a configurable viewer, and a parameter-editor panel.

extras.evidence_summary: All three functions share the MCL name root; MCL has role=viewer, MCLClustering is the compute entry point, and GetMCLEditor (role=editor) provides the parameter UI—a canonical viewer+editor pair.

name: PLS / Multivariate Analysis
description: Partial Least Squares (PLS) regression and multivariate analysis (MVA) that identifies latent factors and builds a linear predictive model maximally correlated with a response variable.

extras.evidence_summary: PLS, topMenuPLS, multivariateAnalysis, MVA demo, and the trainPLSRegression adapter family all describe PLS/MVA in their descriptions; changelog entries v1.4.2 and v1.5.1 confirm ongoing MVA and PLS quadratic regression development.

name: SVM Classification
description: Support Vector Machine (SVM) binary and multi-class classification with four kernel variants—Linear, RBF, Polynomial, and Sigmoid—each exposing train, apply, applicability-check, interactive-check, and visualization adapters.

extras.evidence_summary: All 20 functions share the SVM name suffix, follow an identical train/apply/isApplicable/isInteractive/visualize adapter pattern, and differ only by kernel type—four parallel implementations of the same classifier interface.

name: ANOVA
description: One-way analysis of variance that determines whether a categorical factor has a statistically significant effect on a numeric column, presenting an F-test result with tabbed result views.

extras.evidence_summary: Single function explicitly named ANOVA; changelog v1.4.6 describes F-test description update and tabbed results UI, confirming it as a distinct user-facing analysis.

name: KNN Missing Value Imputation
description: Fills missing values in numeric columns using the k-nearest neighbors algorithm, available as both a column-level dialog and a whole-table batch operation.

extras.evidence_summary: Both functions share the 'KNN impute' name and describe missing-value imputation via KNN; a dedicated help page confirms this as a documented user-facing capability.

name: Linear Regression
description: Ordinary least-squares linear regression model adapter with train, apply, and applicability-check functions for use in the platform's predictive modelling pipeline.

extras.evidence_summary: Four functions share the LinearRegression suffix following the platform's standard train/apply/isApplicable/isInteractive ML adapter pattern.

name: Softmax (Logistic) Classification
description: Multi-class softmax classifier adapter with train, apply, and applicability-check functions for use in the platform's predictive modelling pipeline.

extras.evidence_summary: Four functions share the Softmax suffix following the platform's standard train/apply/isApplicable/isInteractive ML adapter pattern.

name: XGBoost
description: Gradient-boosted tree ensemble (XGBoost) model adapter with train, apply, and applicability-check functions for use in the platform's predictive modelling pipeline.

extras.evidence_summary: Four functions share the XGBooster suffix following the platform's standard ML adapter pattern; changelog v1.4.9 references an ML library update that includes XGBoost.

name: Pareto Front / Multi-Objective Optimization
description: Identifies Pareto-optimal rows across conflicting numeric objectives and displays the trade-off frontier in a dedicated interactive Pareto front viewer.

extras.evidence_summary: Both functions share the 'Pareto front' name (one is the app, one is a viewer role); changelog v1.4.11 explicitly describes the simultaneous release of the viewer and the optimization app.

name: Probabilistic Multi-Parameter Optimization (pMPO)
description: Trains probabilistic MPO desirability models from numeric or categorical columns and exports them as MPO profiles, with an interactive app UI and synthetic dataset generator.

extras.evidence_summary: All three functions share the Pmpo name root; changelog v1.4.12 and v1.5.0 describe the pMPO training app, dataset generation, and model export as a single evolving feature.

name: EPS File Preview
description: Renders Encapsulated PostScript (EPS) files directly inside Datagrok using a built-in file viewer powered by UDOC.js. Users can open any .eps file from the file browser and see a rendered preview without leaving the platform.

extras.evidence_summary: The sole registered function (fileViewer role, extension 'eps'), the readme, the UDOC.js credits, and the initial-release changelog entry all directly describe the same single capability: in-platform EPS rendering.

name: Excalidraw Sketcher App
description: An interactive whiteboard application powered by Excalidraw embedded as a Datagrok view, accessible from the Misc browser path. Allows users to create and edit hand-drawn-style diagrams directly inside the platform.

extras.evidence_summary: The 'Excalidraw' app-role function and the changelog entry 'Excalidraw sketcher integration' share the same name root and directly describe the embedded sketching application.

name: Excalidraw File Viewer
description: A Datagrok file-viewer extension that renders .excalidraw files in-platform when opened from the file browser, without requiring a separate app launch.

extras.evidence_summary: The fileViewer-role function declares ext='excalidraw' and description='Excalidraw viewer', clearly scoping it to file-based rendering as a distinct capability from the interactive app.

name: PDF Viewer
description: Lets users preview and open PDF files directly inside Datagrok via a dedicated file viewer and file handler.

extras.evidence_summary: previewPdf (fileViewer) and viewPdf (fileHandler ext=pdf) share the 'pdf' format tag and are jointly documented by the File Previews guide and the 1.0.0 changelog entries.

name: DOCX Viewer
description: Lets users preview and open DOCX (Word) files inside Datagrok via a dedicated file viewer and file handler.

extras.evidence_summary: previewDocx (fileViewer) and viewDocx (fileHandler ext=docx) share the 'docx' format tag and are recorded together in the 1.0.0 changelog.

name: RTF Viewer
description: Provides in-browser preview of RTF (Rich Text Format) files as a Datagrok file viewer.

extras.evidence_summary: previewRtf is the sole function registered with fileViewer='rtf'; no dedicated changelog entry or handler exists yet.

name: TeX Viewer
description: Provides in-browser preview of LaTeX (.tex) files as a Datagrok file viewer, added in v1.3.1.

extras.evidence_summary: previewTex (fileViewer='tex') and the v1.3.1 changelog entry 'Added support of TEX files' directly correspond.

name: Interactive Function Chain Designer
description: A visual app for composing Datagrok functions into executable pipelines. Users drag and drop functions onto a canvas, connect them, and run the chain interactively.

extras.evidence_summary: func:Flow:Flow is tagged 'app' with description 'Interactive function chain designer'; the readme and changelog entry both reference the FuncFlow view and its drop-handler.

name: Flow Definition File Viewer

name: Genomic Data Viewer

name: Genome Config Validation

name: Map Viewer

name: Geocoding

extras.evidence_summary: All three functions share the 'GIS geocoding' name prefix and description, covering the full geocoding use case (forward, reverse, batch).

name: KML/KMZ File Support
description: Built-in file viewer and handler for KML and KMZ geographic markup files, letting users open and inspect these formats directly in Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: Viewer and handlers share the kml/kmz extension metadata and the 'gisK[M]L/KMZ' name prefix, forming a coherent file-format support unit.

name: GeoJSON/TopoJSON File Support
description: Built-in file viewer and handler for GeoJSON and TopoJSON formats, enabling direct opening of standard web-mapping data files.

extras.evidence_summary: Viewer and handler share the geojson/topojson extension metadata and the 'gisGeoJSON' name prefix; changelog v1.2.1 explicitly narrowed scope to geo/topo JSON.

name: Area / Census Info Panel
description: A context panel that shows census and geographic area information for the currently selected data row, surfacing demographic details derived from coordinates.

extras.evidence_summary: gisAreaWidget (role=widgets,panel) displays census data fetched by getCensusInfo; getCensusInfoTest UI is a dev harness for that API; the three info-panel docs describe the platform mechanism the widget uses.

name: AI Documentation Help

name: AI Function Execution

name: Pinned Columns
