extras.evidence_summary: addPinnedColumn is the sole entry-point; three changelog entries cover pinned-column restoration, fixes, and the 1.8.0 removal notice referencing GROK-1255.

name: WebGPU-Accelerated Grid Rendering
description: Provides GPU-accelerated rendering of grid cell content via the WebGPU API, with availability detection and render-validity checks enabling graceful fallback on unsupported hardware.

extras.evidence_summary: All four functions share the '_scWebGPU' / 'isWebGPU' naming prefix and relate to WebGPU detection and scene-level rendering; changelog entry 'Updated WebGPU cache invalidation logic' in 1.8.0 confirms active development.

name: Spotlight
description: A dashboard widget that surfaces recently-viewed and recently-shared platform entities, giving users a fast way to resume work and discover shared content.

extras.evidence_summary: The Spotlight dashboard widget (role:dashboard) is backed by the MostRecentEntities and RecentlySharedWithMe queries that supply its entity feed.

name: Welcome View
description: The home/activity dashboard view that greets users on startup, aggregating recent work and platform activity into a single landing screen.

extras.evidence_summary: welcomeView outputs a view; changelog entry GROK-19524 explicitly names 'Welcome View: Activity Dashboard' and describes a startup regression fix.

name: Dashboard Widgets
description: A collection of embeddable dashboard widgets — community forum feed, web-page embed, custom HTML, and KPI metric display — for composing informational home screens.

extras.evidence_summary: All four functions share role:widget / role:dashboard and produce widget outputs for dashboard composition; the forum doc is linked via the Community widget.

name: Power Search
description: A platform-wide search provider with AI-powered query interpretation and entity similarity search, accessible via the global search bar.

extras.evidence_summary: The role:searchProvider function is explicitly named 'Power Search' in changelog entries that describe AI query support and entity similarity search additions.

name: Formula Lines
description: Adds formula-driven reference lines and bands to viewers, with a visual editor for defining, validating, and editing formula expressions directly from viewer context menus.

extras.evidence_summary: Formula, formulaLinesEditor, and isFormulaColumn share the 'formula' name root; five changelog entries and two dedicated help docs explicitly describe Formula Lines validation, editing, and filter support.

name: Add New Column
description: An interactive dialog for appending a computed column to a dataframe by authoring a formula expression, with auto-complete, type checking, and validation.

extras.evidence_summary: addNewColumn (input: funccall) is the core function; six changelog entries across multiple versions all reference 'Add new column dialog' by name for formula, validation, and type fixes.

name: Data Enrichment
description: Augments dataframe columns with external data by matching on a key column, supporting catalog browsing, data sync, and cross-connection lookups.

extras.evidence_summary: runEnrichment (role:transform, multi-parameter including dataframe and connection strings) is the sole dedicated function; v1.8.0 opens with an 'Enrichments:' section covering data sync and catalog support.

name: File Viewers
description: In-platform rendering of Markdown (.md / .mdx) files and Excel (.xlsx) spreadsheets without leaving the Datagrok UI.

extras.evidence_summary: Both functions carry role:fileViewer / role:fileHandler and together cover the two most common non-tabular file formats users encounter.

name: Viewer Gallery
description: A browsable gallery for discovering and adding viewers to a table view, automatically filtering out specialty viewers incompatible with the active dataset.

extras.evidence_summary: viewerDialog and viewerGallery share the 'Viewer Gallery' name root; GROK-19533 and GROK-19005 explicitly name the Viewers Gallery in bug-fix entries.

name: Cron Input
description: A value-editor widget for cron-syntax scheduling expressions, used when configuring timed jobs and automations in the platform.

extras.evidence_summary: cronInput carries role:valueEditor and semType:cron; the changelog entry explicitly introduces 'Cron Input: Introduced new cron schedule input widget'.

name: Compare Columns
description: A utility that compares two or more dataframe columns side-by-side to highlight differences and statistical similarities.

extras.evidence_summary: compareColumns is a standalone function whose name unambiguously signals column comparison; no tags or changelog entries provide additional signals.

name: Windows Manager
description: An autostart service that manages platform window layout, docking, and lifecycle so user sessions resume in a consistent spatial arrangement.

extras.evidence_summary: windowsManager has role:autostart and description 'Windows Manager', indicating persistent window-management behavior registered at startup.

name: Preclinical Case Application
description: The main Datagrok app entry point for exploring preclinical trial studies, providing a tree-based navigation panel and named study views.

extras.evidence_summary: The app-role function, its tree-browser companion, and the test helper that opens named views all implement the same study-navigation application shell.

name: XPT / SAS Data Import
description: Reads SAS transport (.xpt) files and parses their contents into Datagrok tables, enabling SEND/SDTM dataset ingestion.

extras.evidence_summary: The fileHandler registered for the .xpt extension and the ReadSas Python script share the same data-ingestion purpose for SAS transport files.

name: CDISC CORE Validation
description: Runs the CDISC CORE rules engine against SEND datasets and renders rule violations inline in the data grid.

extras.evidence_summary: The validation function and the rule-violation cell renderer are both named after the CDISC/SDISC standard and together form an end-to-end validation workflow.

name: Study Measurements Visualization
description: Custom grid cell renderers for displaying combined lab measurements and their y-axis labels within preclinical study data views.

extras.evidence_summary: Both are cellRenderer-role functions that produce grid_cell_renderer outputs for measurement data and axis labeling, and naturally pair in a study data table.

name: Prism File Import
description: Registers a file handler for .prism (GraphPad Prism) files, allowing users to open and import them directly into the Datagrok platform.

extras.evidence_summary: importPrism carries role=fileHandler and ext=prism, directly implementing .prism file opening; the readme documents overall package usage.

name: Prism File Preview
description: Provides an in-place file viewer for .prism files, rendering a preview view when browsing or selecting Prism files without a full import.

extras.evidence_summary: previewPrism carries role=fileViewer and fileViewer=prism, purpose distinct from the importer — it surfaces a view for in-browser file inspection.

name: Compound Info Panel
description: Displays a PubChem-sourced information panel for a selected compound, surfacing physicochemical properties and metadata directly in the Datagrok sidebar.

extras.evidence_summary: Single panel function under 'Databases | PubChem | Info' with direct support from the package readme and the platform info-panels guide.

name: Chemical Structure Search
description: Three PubChem-backed search widgets (Substructure, Similarity, Identity) that let users query the PubChem database for related compounds from within Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: Three functions share the 'Databases | PubChem | {Search type}' menu path and panel role, and multiple changelog entries track fixes and enhancements specifically to these search widgets.

name: Chemical Identifier Conversion
description: Converter functions that translate between PubChem CIDs, InChI Keys, SMILES, and IUPAC names by calling the PubChem REST API.

extras.evidence_summary: All three functions carry the 'converter' role and operate by fetching a single alternative identifier from PubChem; changelog entries document the addition and bug-fixing of exactly these functions.

name: In-Browser Python Scripting
description: Runs Python scripts directly in the browser via WebAssembly using the Pyodide engine, eliminating server round-trips and registering 'pyodide' as a first-class Datagrok script language.

extras.evidence_summary: initPyodide bootstraps the WASM runtime and pyodideLanguageHandler is the registered scriptHandler routing all 'pyodide'-language scripts; the broad scripting docs describe the user experience built on top.

name: Script Vectorization
description: Automatically generates vectorized (columnar) code from element-wise Python scripts, enabling efficient batch processing of DataFrame columns without manual looping.

extras.evidence_summary: makeVectorCode generates vectorized column code; the Calc Column scripts demonstrate and test that vectorization path, and the v1.0.0 changelog explicitly mentions 'scripts vectorization for scripts with language set to pyodide'.

name: Primitive Type Marshaling
description: Converts Python primitive values (bool, int, float, string, datetime, map, blob, column list) to and from JavaScript types, enabling seamless data exchange between Pyodide scripts and the Datagrok runtime.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts named after each primitive type (Bool, Int, Double, String, Date, Map, Blob, Column List) plus SimpleInputs and DatetimeTest pairs form a coherent type-marshaling test suite, corroborated by the 'Complex data types' scripting guide.

name: DataFrame Input/Output
description: Passes Datagrok DataFrames into and out of Pyodide scripts, allowing Python code to read, transform, and return tabular data without serialization overhead.

extras.evidence_summary: EditDF*, DataFrameInputCall*, Empty Dataframe, and Single Df scripts all handle DataFrames as inputs or outputs; the v1.2.1 changelog fixes a bug in 'code generation for dataframes', confirming this as a distinct feature axis.

name: FuncCall Interop
description: Allows Pyodide scripts to invoke other Datagrok FuncCalls synchronously (via run_sync / execFuncCall), enabling Python-scripted orchestration of server-side or JS functions.

extras.evidence_summary: All four *Call* scripts test invoking external FuncCalls from within pyodide scripts, directly matching the v1.3.0 changelog entry 'Allow calling FuncCalls via run_sync and execFuncCall'.

name: Script Package Dependencies
description: Supports declaring Python package dependencies for Pyodide scripts via the meta.dependencies annotation, automatically loading the required packages before script execution.

extras.evidence_summary: The Deps Test script exercises the meta.dependencies mechanism introduced in v1.3.0 to auto-load Python packages inside the WASM sandbox.

name: Graphics Output
description: Renders visual output (charts, plots) produced by Pyodide scripts directly in the Datagrok UI, returning a graphics output column as the result.

extras.evidence_summary: The Graphics script is explicitly described as 'graphics output column input' and maps directly to the 'Visualize output data' scripting guide.

name: Python Script Runtime
description: Provides a server-side Python execution environment for running Python scripts within Datagrok, with built-in pandas support for reading and writing tabular data.

extras.evidence_summary: The info function, basic sample scripts (A+B, pandas), readme, and the Python API / Docker-app developer guides all describe the same core capability: executing Python code server-side.

name: RDKit Cheminformatics
description: Demonstrates RDKit-based cheminformatics computations (Gasteiger charge calculation) runnable as Python scripts on the Datagrok server.

extras.evidence_summary: Script description explicitly names RDKit and targets cheminformatics property calculation.

name: Sklearn Predictive Modeling
description: Provides a scikit-learn linear regression sample that integrates with Datagrok's predictive modeling toolkit, enabling Python-backed ML models.

extras.evidence_summary: Script description explicitly states compatibility with the Datagrok predictive modeling toolkit.

name: Scripting Framework
description: Platform-wide scripting environment with a built-in editor, REST invocation, advanced input/output handling, UI customization, and output visualization — applicable to all scripting languages including Python.

extras.evidence_summary: All scripting guide pages share the /help/compute/scripting/ path prefix and describe the platform-wide script execution framework that Python scripts run within.

name: Info Panels
description: Developer guide for creating and registering context-sensitive info panels in Datagrok, including a reference of supported pane types and a how-to guide.

extras.evidence_summary: Four info-panel docs cluster by shared URL path and audience; they are general platform docs pulled in by the enrichment process, not Python-specific.

name: De Novo Molecule Generation
description: Generates novel small molecules from a seed ligand using the REINVENT4 deep-learning framework, exposed through a dedicated editor UI and the top-level menu; results are returned as a dataframe for downstream analysis.

extras.evidence_summary: All named functions share the 'Reinvent' root or carry the hitDesignerFunction role (molecule generation from seed ligand), and the sole changelog entry confirms this is the plugin's entire purpose.

name: Retrosynthesis Path Analysis
description: Runs AiZynthFinder (via Celery) on a selected molecule and renders the resulting synthetic routes as an interactive Chemistry info panel. Supports user-specific configuration, request cancellation, and saving results to the workspace.

extras.evidence_summary: The panel widget, all three plugin-specific docs, and every changelog entry converge on a single theme: AiZynthFinder-driven retrosynthetic route generation surfaced as a Chemistry info panel.

name: Retrosynthesis Demo
description: A standalone demo application that lets users generate retrosynthesis paths directly, making the feature discoverable without navigating to a molecule's Chemistry panel.

extras.evidence_summary: Single function named 'Retrosynthesis Demo' with description 'Generate retrosynthesis paths' — clearly a standalone demo entry point separate from the panel widget.

name: Revvity Signals Integration App
description: A Datagrok application (browsed from the Chem menu) that connects to the Revvity Signals registration platform, providing an interactive tree browser and a unified entry point to Revvity data.

extras.evidence_summary: The 'Revvity Signals' function (role=app, browsePath=Chem), its tree-browser companion, and the init function share the package name root and together form the application entry point.

name: Entity Search
description: Searches the Revvity Signals registry for chemical entities by text and filter criteria, optionally including chemical structures, and retrieves individual structures by ID.

extras.evidence_summary: The three functions share an explicit 'Search Entities' / 'Get Structure' naming root and all retrieve chemical entity data from Revvity Signals into a dataframe.

name: Metadata and Vocabulary Lookup
description: Provides enumeration and autocomplete helpers for Revvity Signals metadata — users, libraries, tags, and field-level terms — used to populate search filters and UI dropdowns.

extras.evidence_summary: Get Users, Get Libraries, Get Tags, Search Terms, and Get Terms For Field all return metadata lists from Revvity Signals and share the 'Get/Search' prefix pattern as lookup helpers for filters.

name: ETL Pipeline to MolTrack
description: A Python ETL script that exports Revvity Signals libraries, processes CSV files, and prepares chemical compound data for ingestion into MolTrack.

extras.evidence_summary: The Revvity ETL script is explicitly described as a batch pipeline to MolTrack, forming a distinct data-movement capability separate from the interactive search functions.

name: Statistical Hypothesis Testing
description: Demonstrates parametric and non-parametric statistical tests (Welch's t-test, ANOVA, KS test, mixed-effects models) across Python, R, and Julia. Helps users compare group means and test distributional assumptions.

extras.evidence_summary: All members share the explicit hypothesis-testing theme (t-test, ANOVA, KS, LMER) and are the only scripts whose description or name names a statistical test.

name: Multivariate Analysis and Predictive Modelling
description: Covers dimensionality reduction (PCA, LDA, PLS), regression (linear, SVM), dose-response curve fitting, and missing-value imputation (kNN, Datawig). All implemented in R or Python.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts share multivariate/ML methodology — dimensionality reduction, discriminant analysis, regression, and imputation — grouped by analytical purpose rather than language.

name: Scientific Visualization
description: Provides scatter, contour, surface, ternary, and financial chart scripts in R, Python, and Julia, demonstrating cross-language plotting against Datagrok dataframes.

extras.evidence_summary: Scripts are named purely by chart type (scatter, contour, surface, ternary, financial), all output a plot as their primary artefact.

name: Time-Series Analysis and Signal Processing
description: Demonstrates ACF, ARIMA forecasting, time-series decomposition, IIR filtering, CWT scalograms, and spectrogram analysis in R and Python, covering the full signal/time-series workflow.

extras.evidence_summary: ARIMA, decomposition, ACF, scalogram, spectrogram, and IIR filter all operate on sequential/temporal data; Ulmo is a hydrology time-series data library.

name: Image Analysis
description: Showcases Python-based image workflows: cell segmentation via Watershed, EXIF metadata extraction, and deep-learning-based image classification (EfficientNet).

extras.evidence_summary: All three scripts explicitly work with image data (pixel arrays / image files) and have no overlap with other analytical themes.

name: Natural Language Processing
description: Demonstrates text analytics in R: sentiment classification (emotions and polarity) and spell-checking using renv-managed packages.

extras.evidence_summary: Both scripts process natural language text — sentiment analysis and spell-checking are canonical NLP tasks.

name: Clinical Health Formulas
description: Provides reference implementations of BMI (Body Mass Index) in Python and BSA (Body Surface Area) in R as minimal, single-formula scripting examples for clinical use.

extras.evidence_summary: Both scripts compute a single clinical anthropometric formula and share description patterns ('Body Mass/Surface Area'), making them a natural pair.

name: Multi-Language Scripting Demos
description: Simple 'hello-world'-level scripts in Python, R, Julia, JavaScript (Node.js), and Octave that each duplicate a string, demonstrating that Datagrok can execute scripts in all supported languages.

extras.evidence_summary: The four *Dup scripts share identical purpose ('Duplicates a string in <Lang>') and together with Octave Script 2 cover every non-browser scripting language supported by Datagrok.

name: JavaScript UI Scripting
description: Shows how to write browser-side Datagrok scripts in JavaScript: value lookup on a dataframe (cars), UI embedding (Function Input), choice inputs (inputChoice), and client-side validation (validateEmail).

extras.evidence_summary: All four scripts are in the 'javascript' language and demonstrate UI-level patterns (value lookup, embedding, choice input, client validation) unique to browser-side scripting.

name: Pyodide (In-Browser Python) Scripting
description: Three Scottish hills dataset examples — scatter plot, ridge plot, and contour plot — running Python entirely in the browser via Pyodide, demonstrating zero-server-side compute scripting.

extras.evidence_summary: All three scripts share language='pyodide', share the Scottish hills dataset, and were added together in the '1.2.0 Pyodide samples' changelog entry.

name: Script Environment Management
description: Demonstrates how Datagrok manages Python and R package environments: GlomEnv* scripts test environment isolation modes (default, global, inplace), while PythonLibs and ListPackages enumerate installed packages.

extras.evidence_summary: The GlomEnv* trio shares a name root ('GlomEnv') and tests environment isolation variants; PythonLibs and ListPackages both enumerate installed packages in their respective runtimes.

name: Microsoft SQL Server Connector Demo
description: Ten sample queries against an MSSQL Northwind database showcasing every parameter annotation type (int, double, datetime, string pattern, string choices) alongside unfiltered and order-based queries.

extras.evidence_summary: All queries share connection='MSSQLNorthwind'; the ByInt/ByDouble/ByDatetime/ByStringChoices naming explicitly exercises every parameter type covered in the func-params-annotation guide.

name: PostgreSQL Connector Demo
description: Ten sample queries against PostgreSQL Northwind and World databases, including parameterized, lookup, and join-style queries, demonstrating the full range of PostgreSQL connector capabilities.

extras.evidence_summary: Queries share PostgresNorthwind or PostgresWorld connections; together they cover simple selects, parameterized filters, lookup, and join queries against PostgreSQL.

name: ClickHouse Connector Demo
description: Seven sample queries against a ClickHouse Northwind database, mirroring the PostgreSQL Northwind set, to demonstrate ClickHouse connectivity and parameterized queries in Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: All queries share connection='ClickHouseNorthwind' and are named with the 'click house' prefix, forming a self-contained ClickHouse connector demonstration set.

name: RDBMS Connector Demos (Access, MariaDB, MySQL, Oracle)
description: Lightweight Orders and Products query pairs for Microsoft Access, MariaDB, MySQL, and Oracle, providing minimal connectivity demonstrations for each of these RDBMS backends.

extras.evidence_summary: Each database (Access, MariaDB, MySQL, Oracle) contributes exactly an Orders+Products pair against its Northwind connection, making them structurally parallel minimal-demo sets.

name: Oligo Sequence Translation
description: Translates oligonucleotide sequences between notation formats accepted by different synthesizers, with format validation and a monomer-weight-code mapping for modifications.

extras.evidence_summary: Oligo Translator app, translateOligonucleotideSequence, getTranslationHelper, validateSequence, and demoOligoTranslator share the 'Oligo Translator' name root, are grouped under the 'Peptides | Oligo Toolkit' browse path, and together implement the full translation pipeline.

name: Oligo Modification Pattern Design
description: Provides an interactive designer for specifying and visualising chemical modification patterns on oligonucleotide sequences.

extras.evidence_summary: Oligo Pattern app and its demo function share the 'Oligo Pattern' name root under the Oligo Toolkit browse path; the demo description explicitly references 'modification pattern'.

name: Oligo Duplex Rendering
description: Renders siRNA/ASO duplex views in grid cells and property panels, showing modifications, lengths, conjugates, and full sense/antisense molecular structures exportable as SDF.

extras.evidence_summary: oligoNucleotideCellRenderer, Oligo-Nucleotide widget, Oligo Structures widget, and the HELM-to-OligoNucleotide helpers all implement the duplex rendering pipeline; notation provider functions supply the semantic tagging that drives the renderer.

name: PolyTool Notation to Molfile Conversion
description: Converts sequences expressed in custom monomer notations to molfile (SDF) chemical structures, with a rule manager for defining synthesis transformations on modified and synthetic monomers.

extras.evidence_summary: polyToolConvert, polyToolConvert2, getPolyToolConvertEditor, and createMonomerLibraryForPolyTool share the 'polyTool' prefix and convert custom notation to molfiles; the majority of the changelog from v1.5.0 onward tracks PolyTool rule management and synthetic-monomer handling.

name: HELM Sequence Enumeration
description: Enumerates HELM sequences by substituting specified positions with selected monomers (including natural amino acids), generating a new table of sequence variants with optional molfile output.

extras.evidence_summary: HELM Enumerator app, polyToolEnumerateHelm, and the two Enumerate Single HELM Sequence functions all generate variant tables by enumerating HELM positions; grouped under the 'Peptides | PolyTool' browse path.

member_ids: func:SequenceTranslator:Enumerate Single HELM Sequence with natural amino acids

name: Chem Enumeration
description: Enumerates molecules using fragment substitutions at specified positions or via reaction enumeration (Zip or Cartesian product of cores and R-group lists), producing a molecule table.

extras.evidence_summary: Chem Enumerator app, polyToolEnumerateChem, and chemEnumerateReactions enumerate at the chemical (small-molecule) level using fragment/R-group substitution; grouped under the 'Chem | PolyTool' browse path.

name: Sequence Combination
description: Combines separate sequence sets or dual strands (sense + antisense) into unified tables or linked oligo representations for downstream analysis.

extras.evidence_summary: Combine Sequences, linkStrands, and the Oligo Enumerator dialog all combine or link multiple oligonucleotide strands or sequence sets; changelog 1.9.1 explicitly describes the 'Combination of sequence sets dialog'.

name: Signals ELN App Browser
description: Provides a Datagrok application and tree-browser sidebar panel for navigating and accessing data from the Revvity Signals electronic lab notebook. Users can browse ELN entries without leaving the Datagrok platform.

extras.evidence_summary: Both functions implement the same ELN integration — one registers the top-level app view (browsePath: Chem), the other the tree-browser sidebar panel — and the package readme documents the same capability.

name: SQLite File Import
description: Lets users open .sqlite database files directly in Datagrok, exposing each table as a dataframe. The integration runs client-side via the sql.js WASM library with no server round-trip required.

extras.evidence_summary: The single fileHandler (importSQLite), its WASM init (sqlJsInit), all three docs, and the inaugural changelog entry all describe the same capability: reading .sqlite files into the platform.

name: Patent Substructure Search
description: Search a locally deployed SureChEMBL database for patents whose compounds match a given substructure query. Exposed both as a callable function and a context-panel widget surfaced when a molecule is selected.

extras.evidence_summary: Function, panel widget, and backing SQL query all carry the 'substructure' name root; the SureChEMBL info-panel doc covers both search types and is the sole user-facing help page for this plugin.

name: Patent Similarity Search
description: Search a locally deployed SureChEMBL database for patents containing compounds similar to a query molecule, with a configurable similarity threshold. Exposed as a standalone function and a context-panel widget.

extras.evidence_summary: Function, panel widget, and backing SQL query all carry the 'similarity' name root and a numeric threshold input that distinguishes them from the substructure variant.

name: Tableau Workbook (.twb) File Integration
description: Enables Datagrok to open and preview Tableau .twb workbook files by registering a file-import handler and an in-browser file viewer for the format.

extras.evidence_summary: Both functions share the 'twb' extension target and together cover the full fileHandler + fileViewer lifecycle for Tableau workbooks; the readme documents this combined capability.

name: Neural Network Model Training and Inference
description: Lets users train browser-side neural networks via TensorFlow.js on a dataframe column with configurable architecture and hyperparameters, then apply the trained model to new data for in-browser prediction.

extras.evidence_summary: trainNN, applyNN, and isApplicableNN share the 'NN' name root and together implement Datagrok's standard ML model lifecycle interface (train → applicability check → predict).

name: Neural Network Visualization
description: Renders an interactive visual representation of a trained TensorFlow.js neural network for a selected input/output column pair, giving users insight into model structure and activations.

extras.evidence_summary: visualizeNN accepts a trained model object alongside two columns and produces a visualization, making it a clearly separate presentational capability from training and inference.

name: Tutorial Framework & Learning App
description: The interactive step-by-step tutorial engine and Learn widget that guides users through Datagrok features; also provides the developer API for authoring new tutorials.

extras.evidence_summary: The four lifecycle functions (app, widget, autostart, init) are the tutorial engine itself; the write-tutorials dev guide and internal tutorial docs directly document this framework.

name: Interactive Demo App
description: A browseable gallery app that showcases major Datagrok capabilities through live, runnable demos organized in a tree hierarchy.

extras.evidence_summary: All four functions share the 'Demo'/'demoApp' name root and together implement the Demo app entry point, browser widget, tree browser, and hierarchy API.

name: Viewer Demo Gallery
description: Individual runnable demos for every built-in Datagrok viewer (scatter plot, histogram, bar/pie/line charts, 3D, network, density, PC plot, tree map, etc.) hosted inside the Demo app.

extras.evidence_summary: All 23 functions share the 'Demo' suffix and each wraps a single Datagrok viewer, forming a cohesive gallery of viewer demonstrations inside the Demo app.

name: EDA & Visualization Tutorials
description: Interactive tutorials that teach users data exploration with Datagrok viewers (scatter plot, filters, grid, trellis, dashboards, sticky metadata, embedded viewers).

extras.evidence_summary: All tutorials cover interactive data analysis and viewer use, matching the 'dataAnalysisTrack' property; the dashboards and data-tools internal docs directly accompany them.

name: Data Transformation Tutorials
description: Tutorials covering calculated columns and data aggregation to teach users how to reshape and enrich datasets within Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: Both tutorials align to the 'dataTransformation' track property; the data-manipulation internal guide accompanies them.

name: Machine Learning Tutorials
description: Tutorials walking users through multivariate analysis and predictive modeling workflows in Datagrok.

extras.evidence_summary: Both tutorials map to the 'machineLearningTrack' property and the predictive-modeling internal guide.

name: Data Access Tutorials
description: Tutorial and demos covering data connectors, file manager, and database browsing to teach users how to connect Datagrok to external data sources.

extras.evidence_summary: DataConnectorsTutorial and the two demo functions all address connecting to external sources, matching the 'dataAccessTrack' property.

name: Cheminformatics Tutorials
description: Tutorials covering key cheminformatics workflows: substructure search, activity cliffs, R-group analysis, similarity/diversity search, and virtual screening.

extras.evidence_summary: All five tutorials address molecule-centric workflows and map to the 'cheminformaticsTrack' property; changelog also references 'Cheminformatics tutorials'.

name: Bioinformatics Tutorials
description: Tutorial covering peptide sequence activity-relationship (SAR) analysis, representing the bioinformatics learning track.

extras.evidence_summary: PeptidesSarTutorial maps to the 'bioinformaticsTrack' property; changelog entry 'Added Peptides tutorial' confirms it was added as the bio track entry.

name: Scientific Computing Tutorials
description: Tutorials for scripting, differential equations (Diff Studio), parameter optimization, and sensitivity analysis, covering the scientific computing learning track.

extras.evidence_summary: All four tutorials map to the 'scientificComputing' property; changelog v1.7.3 explicitly adds 'Differential equations, Sensitivity analysis, Parameters optimization' as scientific computing tutorials.

name: Data Grid Tests
description: Automated UI tests covering data grid behaviors including sorting, collections management, color coding, datetime extraction, normalization, and table schema validation.

extras.evidence_summary: All six guides cover internal tests for data grid operations (sorting, schema, color coding, normalization, datetime, collections) sharing the /help/_internal/tests/ URL prefix and the same grid-layer concern.

name: Dialog and UI Controls Tests
description: Automated UI tests for dialogs, input fields, context menus, tags, and viewer property panels.

extras.evidence_summary: Dialogs, input fields, menus, and tags guides share UI-interaction themes; TestViewerForProperties is the live viewer fixture used for property-panel testing.

name: Data Connectivity Tests
description: Automated UI tests for data connectors and export: file connector, web connector, Elastic Search, DB query builder, and data export.

extras.evidence_summary: File connector, web connector, Elastic Search, query-join, and export guides collectively verify the platform's data ingestion and connectivity layer.

name: Data Transformation Tests
description: Automated UI tests for the transformation editor, substructure filter, and the custom filter extension point.

extras.evidence_summary: Transformation editor guide, substructure filter guide, and the testCustomFilter fixture all exercise data-mutation and filtering pipelines.

name: Collaboration and User Management Tests
description: Automated UI tests for user registration, invitations, project browsing, recently-opened views, and platform help.

extras.evidence_summary: Registration, inviting friends, project browser, project gallery, recently-open views, and help guides collectively cover user onboarding and collaboration workflows.

name: Test Infrastructure
description: Shared fixtures, placeholder scripts, and package-level metadata supporting the UITests test suite.

extras.evidence_summary: The Dummy script, readme, credits, and changelog entry are package-level infrastructure items not tied to any specific test domain.

name: Usage Analysis App
description: The main admin application that provides a unified dashboard for platform telemetry, covering events, users, packages, functions, and errors across configurable time ranges.

extras.evidence_summary: The 'Usage Analysis' app function (role:app, browsePath:Admin), its init, dashboard widget, and backing usage/events queries all converge on the same admin entry point documented in the govern/audit help page.

name: User & Session Analytics
description: Tracks unique users, new users, sessions, and per-user event breakdowns, providing aggregate counts, ranked lists, and group-level overviews of platform actors.

extras.evidence_summary: All queries share the 'UniqueUsers', 'Sessions', 'Groups', and 'TopUsers' naming root and track the same subject — human actors and their session-level behaviour on the platform.

name: Error Tracking & User Reports
description: Surfaces and ranks platform errors by frequency, source, and package, and powers the Reports app where users can file, browse, and manage error reports with optional Jira ticket creation.

extras.evidence_summary: The 'Reports' app, its appTreeBrowser, Jira-ticket creation, and all TopErrors/UserReports/SameErrors/ReportDataMigration queries share the error-reporting subject; changelog entries explicitly label improvements to this subsystem.

name: Function Call Analytics
description: Measures how often each registered function is called, by whom, from which source, and how long it takes to execute, powering the Functions tab of the main analytics view.

extras.evidence_summary: All queries share the 'Function' name prefix and measure function invocations, execution time, error rates per function, and per-function user breakdowns.

name: Package Analytics
description: Shows which packages are most used and by whom, when they were installed, and provides a context-pane drilldown with function, log, and audit sub-views per package.

extras.evidence_summary: The 'Package' name prefix is shared by all queries; the packageUsageWidget ties the context-pane and overview queries to a single user-visible component.

name: Data Source & Query Analytics
description: Ranks connections, data sources, and SQL queries by usage frequency and identifies which users and functions rely on each data source.

extras.evidence_summary: All queries cluster around connection, query, and data-source objects with consistent Top*/Count/* naming, powering the data-source tab of the analytics app.

name: Test Track & Quality Dashboards
description: Tracks automated and manual test results across builds, compares benchmark performance between versions, and provides the Test Track admin app to browse and manage test cases.

extras.evidence_summary: The 'Test Track' app, testDashboardsViewer, all Test*/Benchmark*/Builds queries, and changelog entries explicitly labelled 'Test Track' share the CI quality-tracking subject.

name: Service Log Viewer
description: Streams and summarises server-side service logs — actions, errors, sessions, and parameter values — through the Service Logs admin app with real-time tail support.

extras.evidence_summary: The 'Service Logs' app and its appTreeBrowser are directly backed by the 'Log *' query family; both the function and queries share the server-log subject and the admin browsePath.

name: Click Tracking
description: Records and visualises UI element click events across the platform, enabling admins to inspect which interface elements receive the most interaction.

extras.evidence_summary: The 'Click Events' widget and 'GetAggregatedClicks' query were introduced together under GROK-18787 and their naming root 'Click' is consistent across code and changelog.

name: Project Analytics
description: Tracks how often projects are accessed and by how many unique users, broken down by day and period, powering the Projects tab introduced in v2.5.0.

extras.evidence_summary: All four queries share the 'Project' name root and track project-level access patterns; the changelog confirms they power the Projects tab introduced in v2.5.0.

name: DB Infrastructure Diagnostics
description: Exposes internal PostgreSQL statistics (pg_stat_statements, table sizes, schema columns) for database-level performance monitoring by platform administrators.

extras.evidence_summary: Three queries expose raw PostgreSQL internals (pg_stat, system table sizes, schema columns) with no counterpart in the user-facing analytics tabs — a clearly distinct admin/infra diagnostic cluster.

name: Web Component Runtime
description: Provides the core initialization and runtime infrastructure that registers and bootstraps custom HTML elements (web components) within the Datagrok platform. This is the entry point that makes all other web components in the package available.

extras.evidence_summary: The single init function, readme, and credits doc all describe the package-level bootstrap runtime; the initial release and access-policy changelog entries mark its lifecycle milestones.

name: Dock Spawn Web Component
description: Custom element wrapper over the dock-spawn panel and tab system, exposing a web-component API for programmatic panel management including focused-tab restoration across sessions.

extras.evidence_summary: Two changelog entries share the 'dock spawn' / 'dock' naming root and both describe tab/panel API behaviour, forming a coherent dock-management capability.

name: Form Consistency Icons
description: Custom web components that render consistency-state indicator icons inside Datagrok forms, giving users immediate visual feedback on field or record validity.

extras.evidence_summary: Changelog entry explicitly names 'consistency icons in forms' as a distinct UI component tweak, suggesting a dedicated form-feedback web component.

name: Compute2 Integration
description: Web component adaptations that align with the Compute2 computation framework, enabling compute-aware UI elements to function correctly within the updated compute pipeline.

extras.evidence_summary: Single changelog entry explicitly references 'Compute2 related updates', indicating a specific integration surface distinct from general platform-version bumps.

name: Category Filters
description: Provides Single Choice and Multi Choice filters for categorical and multi-value columns, letting users narrow table data by selecting one or multiple category values.

extras.evidence_summary: Both functions share the 'filter' role and target categorical column selection, differing only in single vs multi-value semantics.

name: Table Summary Viewer
description: A viewer that displays an at-a-glance summary of a data table, suitable for dashboards and quick data exploration.

extras.evidence_summary: TableSummary carries a standalone 'viewer' role with no closely related functions in the package.

name: Time Widget
description: A home-page widget that displays the current time, serving as a reference implementation for building dashboard widgets.

extras.evidence_summary: TimeWidget is a canonical home-page widget paired directly with the 'Develop widgets for home page' developer guide.

name: Multi Curve Viewer
description: A viewer for rendering multiple curves in a single chart, documented in the platform help pages.

extras.evidence_summary: Dedicated help page titled 'Multi curve viewer' implies a distinct viewer feature; no matching registered function appears in this package's current metadata snapshot.

name: Custom Value Editors
description: Demonstrates how to register custom input editors for semantic types, allowing plugins to supply specialised UI controls for domain-specific column values.

extras.evidence_summary: fooInput (valueEditor for semType 'foo') and inputDemo share the input/editor theme and together illustrate the custom-editor registration pattern.
